title: podolepis capillaris
intro: Podolepis capillaris, also known as the fire daisy or common cress daisy, is a flowering plant species native to Australia. It is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Podolepis capillaris are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
